question: Where can I find the link to watch the live workshop sessions?

Based on the context provided, you can find the link to watch the live workshop sessions in the following places:

*   **Telegram and Slack:** The video URL will be posted in the announcements channel before the session begins. ([Telegram Link](https://t.me/dezoomcamp))
*   **YouTube:** You can watch the sessions live directly on the [DataTalksClub YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/c/DataTalksClub).

question: Where can I find my entry in the rankings? I can't seem to spot myself.
answer_llm: I don't know.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!

question: How do I change the weird random name that got assigned to my profile?
answer_llm:
To change the random name assigned to your profile, click on the **"Edit Course Profile"** button. 

There, you will see two fields you can update:
* **First field (Display name):** This is your public nickname used for the leaderboard. You can change it to your Slack username, GitHub username, or any nickname of your choice. 
* **Second field (Certificate name):** This is for your official name as it appears on your identification documents. You must fill this out if you do not want the random name (like "Lucid Elbakyan") to appear on your certificate.

question: Is it possible to earn a certificate if I go through the course at my own pace?
answer_llm: No, you cannot earn a certificate in a self-paced mode. You can only get a certificate if you finish the course with a "live" cohort. This is because earning a certificate requires you to peer-review 3 capstone projects, which can only be done while the course is actively running.
answer_orig:
No, you can only get a certificate if you finish the course with a "live" cohort.

We don't award certificates for the self-paced mode. The reason is you need to peer-review 3 capstone(s) after submitting your project.
